title: requests — artisan commands
description: Artisan commands this module ships. Registered by `AccessRequestServiceProvider::registerCommands()`. Audience is `ops` (called by cron + on-call runbooks) or `dev` (local-only).

commands.description: Enumerates access requests. Backed by workflow/approvals filtered on `action_key='access.grants.create'`. `--status`: pending | approved | rejected | expired | cancelled | all. `--tenant`: scope to one tenant. `--older-than`: ISO-8601 duration for `requested_at < now - duration`. Prints a compact table (id, tenant, requester, resource, status, requested_at, expires_at).

Prints a full access-request-focused projection of the underlying approval instance: requester, resource, permissions wanted, reason, resolved approvers with their decisions + timestamps, current status, materialised grant id (if any), full audit_trail from context_json.audit_trail[]. Use for support tickets — the SPA show screen renders the same data.

CLI approver decision. `--as` is the approver's user_id or email; defaults to the current console user resolved via `stancl/tenancy`'s console context. `--comment` is passed through to workflow/approvals. Refused when the caller is not in the requirement's resolved_approvers set (same rule as HTTP). ONLY intended for platform ops in incident scenarios — normal approvals flow through the HTTP endpoint.
CLI approver decision — reject. `--comment` REQUIRED for the SOC 2 audit trail; refuses without one. Same caller restrictions as approve.

Compiles a compliance report. Metrics: submissions count, approval rate, rejection rate, expiration rate, cancellation rate, average time-to-decision (hours), median time-to-decision (hours). Grouped by `--group-by` (tenant | application | resource_type). Formats: summary (human), json, csv. Ran weekly via schedule.json + on-demand for support tickets.
Dispatches CleanupOrphanedRequestsJob. `--dry-run` prints affected instance ids without dispatching — useful for verifying cleanup logic before a first run in production.

Ops runbook. Scans approval_instances where action_key='access.grants.create' AND status='approved' AND context_json.materialised_grant_id IS NULL. These are instances that were approved but whose grant materialisation raised (e.g. transient access/grants outage during ApprovalExecuted). Re-runs AccessRequestGrantMaterializer for each. Idempotent — subsequent successful runs no-op.
Clears the AccessRequestableRegistry cache key (`access-requests:requestable-registry`) so a code change to `#[AccessRequestable]` attributes takes effect without a full `config:cache` clear.
